questionHtml: Saturated calcium hydroxide solution is used to test for carbon dioxide. Calculate the pH of a 2.33 × 10−2 mol dm−3 solution of calcium hydroxide, a strong base.
markschemeHtml:
Alternative 1:

[OH−] = « 2 × 2.33 × 10−2 mol dm−3 =» 0.0466 «mol dm−3» ✓

«[H+] = 1.00×10-140.0466 = 2.15 × 10−13 mol dm−3»

pH = « −log (2.15 × 10−13) =» 12.668 ✓

 

Alternative 2:

[OH−] =« 2 × 2.33 × 10−2 mol dm−3 =» 0.0466 «mol dm−3» ✓

«pOH = −log (0.0466) = 1.332»

pH = «14.000 – pOH = 14.000 – 1.332 =» 12.668 ✓

 

Award [2] for correct final answer.

Award [1 max] for pH =12.367.

meta.title: Saturated calcium hydroxide solution is used to test for carbon dioxide. Calculate the pH of a 2.33 × 10−2 mol dm−3 solution of calcium hydroxide, a strong base.
